name: Remove Cluster Background Gradient using Rolling Ball Algorithm
description: Dense objects like globular clusters present a challenge for precision photometry due to a bright, uneven background glow. Your task is to use AstroImageJ's Rolling Ball background subtraction to flatten the V-band image of Messier 12 (~/AstroImages/gradient_removal/Vcomb.fits). Measure the median values of a 100x100 pixel region over the bright cluster core and a dark region near the edge both before and after applying a rolling ball filter with a radius of 50.0 pixels (configured for a dark background). Save the processed image to ~/AstroImages/gradient_removal/output/Vcomb_flattened.fits and a text report containing the four measured median values to ~/AstroImages/gradient_removal/output/background_report.txt.
